channel.title: Web3 on jamesm.blog
channel.description: Recent content in Web3 on jamesm.blog

item.title: Quantum Computing: A Threat to Bitcoin?
item.description:
Overview

Quantum computing represents one of the most significant theoretical threats to modern cryptography. For Bitcoin, the primary concern lies in the potential for quantum computers to run Shor’s Algorithm, which could efficiently solve the discrete logarithm problem that secures Bitcoin’s public-key cryptography (ECDSA).

The Vulnerabilities

  • ECDSA (Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm) — Currently used to sign Bitcoin transactions. A sufficiently powerful quantum computer could derive a private key from its corresponding public key.
  • Public Key Exposure — While Bitcoin addresses are hashed (providing a layer of protection), the public key is revealed to the network when a transaction is initiated, creating a window of vulnerability before the block is mined.
  • Mining (SHA-256) — Grover’s Algorithm could speed up mining, but this is a quadratic improvement rather than exponential, meaning it could likely be mitigated by increasing network difficulty or hash rates.

Mitigation and Post-Quantum Bitcoin

The Bitcoin community is well aware of these risks. Potential solutions include:

item.title: Polkadot 2026: From Infrastructure to Applications
item.description:
The Pivot Year: Polkadot’s Strategic Shift in 2026

Polkadot has undergone a fundamental transformation in 2025-2026. After years of building infrastructure layers, the ecosystem is making a decisive pivot toward user-facing applications. This isn’t just a narrative shift—it’s embedded in technical upgrades, tokenomics redesigns, and validator economics that reflect a maturing network ready to compete at the application layer.

Timing: This transformation arrives as traditional finance begins acknowledging blockchain infrastructure, and as the broader crypto market cycle approaches a pivotal moment for adoption.

item.title: Gavin Wood
item.description:
About

Dr. Gavin James Wood is an influential English computer scientist and entrepreneur who has fundamentally shaped modern blockchain architecture. As a co-founder of Ethereum, he contributed to the foundational concepts of smart contracts and the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM). Subsequently, he founded Polkadot and Kusama, designing next-generation blockchain ecosystems focused on interoperability and scalability.

Featured Articles

  • Polkadot 2023 Roundup (December 2023)

Video Talks & Interviews

Recent (2023+)

Is Polkadot’s OpenGov Democratic? (November 2023)

item.title: Blockchain & Crypto Resources
item.description:
Curated YouTube Channels

Explore these trusted YouTube channels covering blockchain, cryptocurrencies, Web3, and market analysis.

Educational & Technical

  • Andreas Antonopoulos — One of the most trusted and engaging educators in the Bitcoin industry, explaining blockchain fundamentals with clarity and depth

  • Coin Bureau — Comprehensive informational portal to the cryptocurrency ecosystem with detailed project analysis

  • MMCrypto — In-depth coverage of cryptocurrency and blockchain topics for learners at all levels

Market & Trading

  • Altcoin Daily — Bitcoin, Ethereum, Cardano, NFTs, altcoins, metaverse, mining, trading, and investing insights

item.title: Blockchain & Web3 Courses
item.description:
Overview

Whether you’re a beginner exploring blockchain fundamentals or an experienced developer building decentralized applications, these curated courses provide structured learning paths from foundation concepts to advanced smart contract development.

Learning Resources

Developer-Focused Platforms

  • Blockchain Council

    • Certified Blockchain Developer™ — Professional certification program

  • ChainShot — Interactive bootcamp for blockchain development

  • CryptoZombies — Learn Solidity through interactive gaming

  • LearnWeb3 — Comprehensive Web3 developer education

  • Polkadot Blockchain Academy — Official Polkadot training for developers and founders
